how.title: Three taps from hungry to happy
how.sub: TasteBud turns “what should I order?” into a question you never have to ask again.

steps.title: Tell us what you love
steps.body: A quick, playful taste quiz — sweet or spicy, rich or fresh. Two minutes, no account headaches.

steps.title: We learn your taste
steps.body: Your flavor fingerprint sharpens with every meal you rate — so the picks keep getting more you.

steps.title: Get dishes worth ordering
steps.body: At the table, scan the menu and get your picks — each with a warm “here’s why you’ll love it.”

features.title: A companion with great taste
features.sub: Built around precision and empathy — the useful pick, delivered kindly.

items.title: Swipe to react
items.body: Skip, save, or order with a flick. Reacting to dishes is faster — and far more fun — than reading a wall of menu text.

items.title: Here’s why you’ll love it
items.body: Every recommendation comes with a sensory reason tied to your taste — never a cold “92% match” and nothing else.

items.title: Your flavor fingerprint
items.body: A living taste map that shows where your taste stands today — and how it’s evolving over time.

items.title: Works at any restaurant
items.body: Pick where you are, and TasteBud reads the menu for you — your picks, wherever the night takes you.

items.title: Learns from every meal
items.body: A quick post-meal check-in teaches TasteBud what landed — so tomorrow’s picks are even sharper.

items.title: English & Español
items.body: Fully bilingual from day one, with dietary and allergy filters baked in for everyone at the table.

points.b: A confidence score you can trust

see the spicy, umami and sour notes before the plate arrives.
veggie, spicy and price, surfaced up front.
a sentence in your corner: “umami and spicy notes that match your profile.”

showcase.cta: Try it on your next meal →

items.quote: I’m the person who takes 20 minutes to order. TasteBud nailed my pick in 20 seconds — and it was the best thing I’ve eaten all month.
items.name: Lucía R.

items.quote: The little reason under each dish is the magic. It doesn’t just say ‘order this’ — it tells me why, and it’s always right.
items.name: Marco T.

items.quote: Travelling and don’t know the cuisine? Open TasteBud, pick the restaurant, swipe. It feels like having a local friend with great taste.
items.name: Sofía & Dan

items.a: A personalized dining companion. You tell it what you love, it learns your taste, and it recommends the dishes worth ordering — at the restaurant you’re actually at — each with a warm reason why.

items.q: Do I need to be at the restaurant?
items.a: That’s where TasteBud shines. Pick the place you’re at, tell it what you’re in the mood for, and it reads that menu and hands you your picks. You can also browse and save dishes any time.
Yes — set vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, allergies and more during setup, and every recommendation respects them. Dishes are clearly badged for spice, veggie and price.

items.q: How does it get the recommendations right?
items.a: A hybrid engine combines what people with similar taste love with the actual flavor profile of each dish, then tunes to your fingerprint. Every meal you rate makes the next pick sharper.
We’re in a closed beta — a small group of testers is shaping the experience before we open the doors. Request an invite with your email and we’ll bring you in as spots free up.
Request access above. When a spot opens we’ll email you an invite link — then sign in with a magic code (no password) and the taste quiz takes about two minutes.

footer.tagline: Finding your taste, one dish at a time. Transforming the way people enjoy food — innovation and warmth in every meal.

notFound.title: This dish isn’t on the menu.
notFound.body: The page you’re looking for moved or never existed. Let’s get you back to something tasty.

biz.heroSub: Restaurants fly blind on the one thing that decides everything: flavor. You know what sold — never quite why. TasteHub turns the taste graph behind TasteBud into an operating system for your menu, so you can diagnose, design, and decide with the same precision your diners feel on the plate.

problem.title: You can measure every cost in the kitchen except the one that matters most.
problem.p1: A modern restaurant tracks food cost to the cent, labour to the minute, and covers to the seat. Yet the question that quietly decides the night — does this dish actually match the taste of the people I serve? — gets answered by instinct, a chef’s palate, and last month’s sales mix. When a plate stalls, the data tells you it stalled. It never tells you whether it was too bitter for your lunch crowd, undercut by a similar dish three lines up, or simply priced past its perceived value.
problem.p2: TasteBud already speaks the missing language. To recommend dishes to diners, it represents both people and plates as flavor and texture profiles in one shared space. TasteHub points that same engine at your business — so the guesswork in menu design becomes something you can read, test, and improve.

modules.title: Three ways to run your menu on taste
modules.sub: One taste graph, three jobs to be done — diagnose what’s on the menu, design what’s next, and truly know who you cook for.

items.title: Know exactly why a plate isn’t selling
items.lead: Sales reports tell you what underperformed. They never tell you why. TasteHub does.
items.body: TasteHub decomposes every dish into its flavor and texture profile — sweet, sour, salty, bitter, umami, fatty, spicy, plus crunch, cream and chew — and weighs it against the people who actually walk through your door. Instead of a flat “this sold poorly,” you get a ranked, plain-language diagnosis of the root cause.
points: A taste mismatch — the dish sits outside your room’s profile
A single off-putting note or ingredient dragging it down
Cannibalization by a better-matched dish on your own menu
Pricing and menu placement working against it

items.title: Design new dishes that are aligned to sell
items.lead: A creative partner for your kitchen that thinks in flavor, not guesswork.
items.body: Describe a gap — “a lighter summer main under $18” — and the copilot proposes dishes and tweaks tuned to the flavors your diners crave and to what’s rising across the wider taste graph. Before you prep a single portion, it projects how the dish will land: a predicted match distribution across your diner base, an expected popularity score, and a flag for any existing plate it might cannibalize.
points: Generative dish and flavor ideas, tuned to your room
Predicted reception and popularity before you cook
Experiment safely — model a flavor swap and see the lift
Keeps your signature; sharpens what surrounds it

items.title: Understand your diners at the level of taste
items.lead: Stop guessing who you cook for. See the flavor fingerprint of your room.
items.body: TasteHub builds the aggregate taste profile of the people who actually eat with you — and the neighborhood around you — and tracks how it shifts by daypart and season. Lay it over your current menu and the gaps light up: the flavor profiles your diners want that you under-serve, and the ones you over-index on. It’s the difference between a menu built on instinct and one built on demand.
points: Live flavor trends, by daypart and season
Menu coverage vs. real diner demand, side by side
Your room and your neighborhood, benchmarked
Privacy-first — always aggregate, never individual

outcomes.l: diners and dishes in one language

flywheel.title: A taste flywheel that compounds
flywheel.sub: TasteBud and TasteHub are two ends of one loop. The more diners taste, the smarter your menu decisions get.

steps.title: Diners react
steps.body: People use TasteBud to find dishes they love at your restaurant, reacting to plates as they go.

steps.title: Taste becomes data
steps.body: Every reaction sharpens a shared taste graph — dishes and diners described in the same flavor language.

steps.title: TasteHub turns it into decisions
steps.body: You see why dishes win or stall, design new ones with confidence, and align the menu to real demand.

steps.title: The menu gets better
steps.body: Better-matched menus delight more diners — who react more — and the whole loop compounds in your favor.

partners.title: Great food deserves to be found
partners.sub: Whether you run a kitchen or back the teams shaping how people eat — let’s start a conversation.

restaurants.title: For restaurants

points: Spotlight your signature dishes to the right palates
Diagnose underperformers down to the flavor note

Reach diners by taste, not noisy ads

Two products, one widening data moat
Mobile-first and bilingual from day one
A closed beta already shaping real engagement

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
intro: TasteBud (“TasteBud”, “we”, “us”, “our”) provides a personalized dining-recommendation service through our website at tastebud-co.com and our app. This Privacy Policy explains what personal information we collect, how we use and share it, and the choices and rights you have.
By using TasteBud, you agree to the collection and use of information as described here. If you do not agree, please do not use the Service.

p: We collect information you give us directly and information we gather automatically as you use TasteBud, and we keep it to what we need to recommend dishes you’ll love.
items: Information you provide: your email address (to request beta access and to sign in), your answers to the onboarding taste quiz, the dietary restrictions, allergies and exclusions you set, your reactions, ratings and post-meal feedback on dishes, the restaurant or area you choose for recommendations, your language preference, and — if you contact us or use our business form — your name, company, role and message.

Information we collect automatically: basic device and usage data (such as browser and device type, pages viewed and actions taken), and log and diagnostic data used to operate and secure the Service.
We do not collect precise GPS location — you tell us which restaurant or area you’re dining in.
Provide, personalize and improve your dish recommendations and build your evolving taste profile (your “flavor fingerprint”).
Sign you in securely using one-time codes sent to your email (we do not use passwords).
Respond to your requests and send service messages, such as your beta invite and the occasional taste tip.
Keep the Service safe — preventing abuse, debugging, and protecting you and other users.
Improve our recommendation models and develop new features.
Comply with legal obligations and enforce our Terms.

p: TasteBud is powered by automated systems and artificial intelligence. We represent both dishes and your taste as numerical profiles, and we use trusted third-party AI services (currently OpenAI) to generate dish embeddings and the written explanations that accompany each recommendation.

To do this, dish descriptions and de-identified taste features may be processed by these providers. These systems help us recommend food; they do not make legally significant decisions about you, and you can always tell us when a recommendation misses so we can learn.
We do not sell your personal information, and we do not share it with advertisers. We share information only in these limited cases:
items: Service providers: companies that host and operate TasteBud, store our data, send our email, and provide the AI features described above — bound by contracts that limit their use of your data to providing those services.
Aggregated, de-identified insights: we may produce aggregate taste statistics that do not identify you and use them to operate and improve TasteBud and our restaurant-facing product, TasteHub.
Legal and safety: when we believe it is reasonably necessary to comply with law, enforce our Terms, or protect the rights, safety and integrity of our users and the Service.
Business transfers: in connection with a merger, acquisition, financing or sale of assets, subject to this Policy.

p: We use a minimal set of cookies and browser local storage to keep you signed in (authentication tokens) and to remember your language preference (the “tastebud-language” setting). We do not use third-party advertising or cross-site tracking cookies. You can clear these from your browser at any time, though you’ll need to sign in again.

We keep your account information while your account is active. Your taste feedback is weighted with a time decay — older signals gradually fade (over roughly a 21-day half-life) — so your profile reflects your current taste. Beta-waitlist emails are kept until we open to the public or you ask to be removed. We delete or de-identify personal data when it is no longer needed, unless we must retain it to meet legal obligations.

Depending on where you live — including under the EU/UK GDPR, and laws in Latin America and other regions — you may have the right to access, correct, delete or export your personal data, to object to or restrict certain processing, and to withdraw consent at any time. You can exercise these rights, or ask us to delete your account, by emailing us (see “Contact us” below); we’ll respond within the time required by law.

Some dietary and allergy information may be considered sensitive personal data. We process it only to deliver safe, relevant recommendations, and with your consent. You can update or remove it in your profile at any time.

Where the GDPR applies, we rely on the following legal bases: performance of our contract with you (providing recommendations), your consent (for example, joining the waitlist and providing dietary data), and our legitimate interests in operating, securing and improving TasteBud.

TasteBud works with service providers located in the United States and other countries. If you use TasteBud from elsewhere, your information may be transferred to and processed in countries whose data-protection laws differ from your own. Where required, we take steps to protect your information consistent with this Policy.

We protect your information with measures including encryption in transit (HTTPS), passwordless one-time-code sign-in, and access controls. No method of transmission or storage is ever completely secure, so while we work hard to protect your data we cannot guarantee absolute security.

TasteBud is not directed to children under 13 (or the minimum age required in your country), and we do not knowingly collect their personal information. If you believe a child has provided us personal data, please contact us and we will delete it.

We may update this Privacy Policy as TasteBud evolves. We’ll revise the “Last updated” date above and, for material changes, provide additional notice where appropriate. Your continued use of TasteBud after an update means you accept the revised Policy.

terms.title: Terms of Service
intro: These Terms of Service (“Terms”) govern your access to and use of TasteBud’s website, app and related services (together, the “Service”), provided by TasteBud (“TasteBud”, “we”, “us”).
By accessing or using the Service, you agree to these Terms and to our Privacy Policy. If you do not agree, please do not use the Service.

p: You must be at least 13 years old — or the minimum age of digital consent in your country — and able to form a binding contract to use TasteBud. If you are under the age of majority where you live, you may use the Service only with the involvement and consent of a parent or legal guardian.

TasteBud helps you decide what to order by learning your taste and recommending dishes, each with a short explanation. The Service is currently offered as a closed beta, on an “as is” and “as available” basis. Features may change, be interrupted, or be removed, and access may be granted, limited or revoked at our discretion as we develop the product.

TasteBud uses passwordless sign-in: we email you a one-time code. You are responsible for the email account you use to sign in and for activity that happens under your account. Please keep your email secure and let us know promptly if you suspect unauthorized access.

sections.h: 4. Food, allergies and dietary information — please read
p: TasteBud’s recommendations, flavor profiles, dietary and spice badges, and explanations are provided for general informational purposes only and may be incomplete, out of date, or inaccurate. Menus, ingredients, recipes and preparation methods are controlled by the restaurants, not by TasteBud, and can change at any time without notice.

TasteBud does not prepare food and cannot guarantee that any dish is free from a particular ingredient or allergen, or suitable for any specific diet or medical condition. If you have a food allergy, intolerance, or a medical or dietary condition, you must confirm ingredients and preparation directly with the restaurant before ordering or eating, and never rely on TasteBud to avoid an allergen.

You assume full responsibility for your food choices. To the fullest extent permitted by law, TasteBud is not liable for any allergic reaction, illness, injury or other harm arising from food you order or consume.

When you provide taste preferences, dish reactions, ratings, feedback or other content, you grant TasteBud a worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free licence to use, host, store, reproduce and process that content to operate, personalize and improve the Service and to create aggregated, de-identified insights. You confirm you have the right to share it. If you send us suggestions or ideas, we may use them freely and without any obligation to you.
You agree to use TasteBud lawfully and respectfully. In particular, you agree not to:
items: misuse, disrupt, overload, or attempt to gain unauthorized access to the Service or its systems;
scrape, crawl, harvest, or copy data from the Service except as expressly permitted;
reverse engineer, decompile, or attempt to extract our source code, models or underlying data;
use the Service to break the law or to infringe the rights of others; or
submit content that is false, misleading, harmful, or that you do not have the right to submit.

p: The Service — including TasteBud and TasteHub, and their software, models, design, text, graphics, logos and trademarks — is owned by TasteBud and protected by intellectual-property laws. We grant you a limited, personal, non-transferable, revocable licence to use the Service for its intended purpose. All rights not expressly granted are reserved.

TasteBud is independent: we are not affiliated with, do not endorse, and do not operate the restaurants whose menus appear in the Service. Menu information may come from third-party sources and may be inaccurate or out of date. Any interaction or transaction you have with a restaurant or other third party is solely between you and them.

The Service is provided “as is” and “as available”, without warranties of any kind, whether express or implied, including warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, accuracy and non-infringement. We do not warrant that the Service will be uninterrupted or error-free, or that any recommendation will meet your expectations or suit your needs.

To the fullest extent permitted by law, TasteBud and its team will not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential or punitive damages, or for any loss of data, profits or goodwill, or for any harm arising from food you order or consume, arising out of or relating to your use of the Service. Our total liability for any claim relating to the Service will not exceed the greater of the amount you paid us in the 12 months before the claim (which, during the free beta, is zero) or USD 100.

You agree to indemnify and hold harmless TasteBud and its team from any claims, damages, losses and reasonable expenses arising out of your misuse of the Service, your content, or your violation of these Terms or of applicable law.

You may stop using TasteBud at any time. We may suspend or end your access at any time — including for a breach of these Terms or to discontinue the beta. Provisions that by their nature should survive termination (such as the licence you grant for content already provided, disclaimers, limitation of liability and indemnification) will continue to apply.

These Terms are governed by the laws of the jurisdiction in which TasteBud is established, without regard to conflict-of-law rules, and you agree to the exclusive jurisdiction of the competent courts located there — except where the mandatory consumer-protection laws of your country of residence grant you additional rights.

We may update these Terms as the Service evolves. We’ll revise the “Last updated” date above and, for material changes, provide notice where appropriate. By continuing to use TasteBud after an update, you accept the revised Terms.
